assertions.title: Shared dynamics types used by show/context/run

steps.action: Inspect shared dynamics structs in werk-cli/src/dynamics.rs
steps.expected: Single definition site for shared dynamics serialization types
steps.observed: Found shared structs in dynamics.rs only (e.g., DynamicsJson, ContextDynamicsJson, StructuralTensionJson, ResolutionJson, HorizonDriftJson, TensionInfo, MutationInfo).

steps.action: Inspect imports in show/context/run command modules
steps.expected: show/context/run import shared dynamics module types/functions
steps.observed: show.rs, context.rs, and run.rs import from crate::dynamics.

steps.action: Search command modules for duplicate definitions of shared dynamics structs
steps.observed: No matches for shared dynamics struct definitions under src/commands.

assertions.title: DynamicsEngine used for computation

steps.action: Search CLI source for DynamicsEngine usage
steps.observed: No DynamicsEngine references found in werk-cli/src.

steps.action: Inspect CLI source for direct sd_core dynamics function calls
steps.expected: No direct detect_*/predict_* computation calls in CLI
steps.observed: Direct calls are present: compute_all_dynamics in dynamics.rs calls detect_oscillation/detect_compensating_strategy/predict_structural_tendency; tree.rs imports and calls classify_creative_cycle_phase/detect_structural_conflict/predict_structural_tendency directly.

terminalSnapshots: Grep: DynamicsEngine in werk-cli/src -> No matches found.
Grep: detect_oscillation|detect_compensating_strategy|predict_structural_tendency in werk-cli/src -> matches in dynamics.rs:240,265,276 and commands/tree.rs:167.
Grep with context in commands/tree.rs shows direct call: let tendency = predict_structural_tendency(tension, has_conflict, Some(now), None);
Grep with context in commands/tree.rs imports raw functions from sd_core at lines 7-10.

Grep pattern 'use sd_core::' path werk-cli/src/commands/tree.rs with context

assertions.issues: Assertion requires DynamicsEngine-based CLI computation, but no DynamicsEngine usage exists and direct dynamics function calls remain in CLI source.

steps.action: Create isolated horizon tension in namespaced temp workspace and mutate actual progressively toward desired without fully matching desired
steps.observed: Using desired="zzzzzzzz" and actual progression aaaaaaaa -> aaaazzzz -> aaazzzzz -> aazzzzzz -> azzzzzzz produced a non-null resolution object.

steps.expected: resolution includes required_velocity and is_sufficient when horizon exists
steps.observed: resolution object keys include required_velocity and is_sufficient; values observed: required_velocity=1.8562393068403827e-08, is_sufficient=true.

steps.action: Run targeted integration test covering resolution velocity fields

terminalSnapshots: cargo test --manifest-path /Users/moritzbierling/werk/desk/werk/Cargo.toml -p werk --test cross_area_dynamics test_toon_includes_ -- --nocapture -> test_toon_includes_resolution_velocity_fields ... ok.

frictions.description: Initial horizon test scenarios for VAL-CDM-005 produced dynamics.resolution = null (no detected directional gap closure), requiring a deterministic monotonic string-gap progression scenario to surface the resolution object.
frictions.resolution: Used desired='zzzzzzzz' with actual updates aaaaaaaa -> aaaazzzz -> aaazzzzz -> aazzzzzz -> azzzzzzz to guarantee positive gap-reduction direction while keeping desired != actual.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 4 passed (VAL-CDM-001, VAL-CDM-003, VAL-CDM-004, VAL-CDM-005) and 1 failed (VAL-CDM-002: CLI does not use DynamicsEngine and still has direct dynamics function calls).
